what_it_does: Presents a distilled, neutral map of four unresolved design/implementation decision forks and invites the reader to choose which fork to explore or decide, without recommending any option.
observations: YAML frontmatter marks this as a current block-type noticeboard item sourced from claude_code in the dragnet-development channel.
The title labels it 'MAP4' and explicitly notes it is 'not my call'.
It lists four decision dimensions: SCOPE, SPINE, THE GESTURE, and THE ENVELOPE.
It notes the loop-closing scope option may be cheap and high-value regardless of other choices.
The author states they are deliberately not recommending and mentions 'agents were built to disagree'.
It offers to run a focused round or tighter design pass on whichever fork the user selects.
standouts: Explicit neutrality — the file refuses to recommend and frames the decision as the user's.
Four concise forks cover scope, spine, gesture, and envelope architecture.
The scope fork is flagged as likely valuable regardless of the others.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML frontmatter, id, address, type, source, state, title, and a part_of link.
Uses a MAP-prefixed title (MAP4) to identify a decision-mapping artifact.
Frames choices as numbered forks with parenthetical clarifications.
Maintains a facilitator voice rather than an authoritative recommendation.
concerns: Several domain terms are used without local definition (e.g., 'tree + envelope foundation', 'touch-first', 'voice-first', 'the gesture', 'derived-mark').
The claim 'agents were built to disagree' is asserted without context about which agents or their positions.
No decision owner, deadline, or explicit criteria for choosing among forks are provided.
notes: A neutral four-fork decision map that defers all choices to the reader and offers to go deeper on whichever fork is selected.
questions: What is the parent item board://item-1deff1b2 and how does this map fit into it?
What specific system or feature do 'tree + envelope foundation', 'touch-first', 'voice-first', and 'derived-mark' refer to?
Which agents disagreed and what were their respective positions?
What criteria should be used to weigh these forks?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is to distill forks for the user to weigh, and the content does exactly that without recommending.
apparent_themes: Neutral facilitation and deferral to the user
Lean, high-value quick wins versus foundational architecture
intention_signals: To reduce decision complexity by separating interleaved design questions into distinct forks, then prompt the user to commit to one fork for a focused next round.
novelty: The deliberate refusal to recommend combined with a meta-offer to run a focused design pass once the user picks a fork, plus the framing that disagreement among agents is expected.
contribution: A compact, four-axis decision scaffold that preserves the open state of multiple design forks and provides a clear path to escalate one fork into a focused exploration.
summary_for_embedding: A current noticeboard item in the dragnet-development channel that maps four unresolved design/implementation forks (scope, spine, gesture, envelope) without recommending a choice and invites the user to select a fork for deeper exploration.
intention_for_embedding: To help a decision-maker weigh four open design/code forks and choose which one to refine next.
